Tuber melanosporum, commonly known as the black truffle, is a type of fungi belonging to the genus Tuber. It is prized for its earthy, umami flavor and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.

The bioactive constituents of Tuber melanosporum include phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Tuber melanosporum is commonly used in culinary and herbal preparations, including truffle-infused oils, vinegars, and sauces, as well as truffle-based supplements and capsules. It is also used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, and other herbal remedies.
